theorem_1_1 to get the bound Eψ x ≤ ε(...).
  -- We need to prove the conditions (bounds on σ2, etc.) and that ε(...) ≤ ε_total.
  -- Since the values come from a table of floats, we cannot verify the strict inequalities 
  -- and the calculation of ε (which involves integrals) inside the proof kernel easily.
  -- We thus admit the numeric checks.
  refine le_trans (theorem_1_1 Inputs.default (exp log_x0) σ2 c N K ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ x ?_) ?_
  · sorry -- Verify log(exp log_x0) > 1000
  · sorry -- Verify 0.9 < σ2 < 1
  · norm_num -- Verify 2 ≤ c ≤ 30 (c=3)
  · sorry -- Verify N ≥ 1
  · norm_num -- Verify K ≥ 1 (K=100000)
  · rwa [Real.log_exp] -- Verify x ≥ exp log_x0
  · sorry -- Verify calculated ε ≤ ε_total

intro x hx
  let x₀ := exp log_x0
  let c_used : ℝ := 3
  let K_used : ℕ := 100000
  let N_used : ℕ := (Int.ceil (100000 * (σ2 - 0.9))).toNat
  have hx₀ : x ≥ x₀ := by sorry
  have h_log : log x₀ > 1000 := by sorry
  have h_σ2 : σ2 ∈ Set.Ioo 0.9 1 := by sorry
  have h_c : c_used ∈ Set.Icc 2 30 := by sorry
  have h_N : N_used ≥ 1 := by sorry
  have h_K : K_used ≥ 1 := by sorry
  have h_calc : ε Inputs.default x₀ σ2 c_used N_used K_used ≤ ε_total := by sorry
  exact le_trans (theorem_1_1 Inputs.default x₀ σ2 c_used N_used K_used h_log h_σ2 h_c h_N h_K x hx₀) h_calc
